key_points: Force is a push or pull that can change the state of motion or shape of an object
Forces can cause an object to start moving, stop moving, or change direction
Balanced forces act in opposite directions with equal magnitude and do not change motion
Unbalanced forces cause objects to accelerate or change their motion
Friction is a contact force that opposes the motion between two surfaces in contact
examples: A football changes direction when kicked by a player
Two people pushing a box from opposite sides with equal force keep it stationary
A rolling ball gradually stops due to friction with the ground
A magnet pulling a piece of iron without touching it
formulas: Force effect → Change in speed, direction, or shape
exam_tips: Understand the difference between balanced and unbalanced forces
Use diagrams when describing forces acting on objects
Remember that friction always acts opposite to motion
